verses.latin_vulgate: Et afflictus est Jonas afflictione magna, et iratus est :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֥רַע אֶל־יוֹנָ֖ה רָעָ֣ה גְדוֹלָ֑ה וַיִּ֖חַר לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonah was exceedingly troubled, and was angry:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jona pani sen kovin pahaksensa, ja vihastui.
verses.king_james: But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.

verses.latin_vulgate: et oravit ad Dominum, et dixit : Obsecro, Domine, numquid non hoc est verbum meum cum adhuc essem in terra mea ? propter hoc præoccupavi ut fugerem in Tharsis : scio enim quia tu Deus clemens et misericors es, patiens et multæ miserationis, et ignoscens super malitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֨ל אֶל־יְהֹוָ֜ה וַיֹּאמַ֗ר אָנָּ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ הֲלוֹא־זֶ֣ה דְבָרִ֗י עַד־הֱיוֹתִי֙ עַל־אַדְמָתִ֔י עַל־כֵּ֥ן קִדַּ֖מְתִּי לִבְרֹ֣חַ תַּרְשִׁ֑ישָׁה כִּ֣י יָדַ֗עְתִּי כִּ֤י אַתָּה֙ אֵֽל־חַנּ֣וּן וְרַח֔וּם אֶ֤רֶךְ אַפַּ֙יִם֙ וְרַב־חֶ֔סֶד וְנִחָ֖ם עַל־הָרָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he prayed to the Lord, and said: I beseech thee, O Lord, is not this what I said, when I was yet in my own country? therefore I went before to flee into Tharsis: for I know that thou art a gracious and merciful God, patient, and of much compassion, and easy to forgive evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Rukoili Herraa ja sanoi: voi Herra! sepä se on, minkä minä sanoin, kuin minä olin vielä maassani, sentähden minä tahdoin myös Tarsikseen paeta; sillä minä tiedän, että sinä Jumala olet armollinen, laupias, pitkämielinen, suuresta hyvyydestä ja kadut pahaa.
verses.king_james: And he prayed unto the Lord, and said, I pray thee, O Lord, was not this my saying, when I was yet in my country? Therefore I fled before unto Tarshish: for I knew that thou art a gracious God, and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repentest thee of the evil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc, Domine, tolle, quæso, animam meam a me, quia melior est mihi mors quam vita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה קַח־נָ֥א אֶת־נַפְשִׁ֖י מִמֶּ֑נִּי כִּ֛י ט֥וֹב מוֹתִ֖י מֵחַיָּֽי׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And now, O Lord, I beseech thee take my life from me: for it is better for me to die than to live.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ota nyt, Herra, minun sieluni minusta pois; sillä minä kuolisin paremmin kuin eläisin.
verses.king_james: Therefore now, O Lord, take, I beseech thee, my life from me; for it is better for me to die than to live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus : Putasne bene irasceris tu ?
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said: Dost thou think thou hast reason to be angry?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra sanoi: luuletkos oikein vihastunees?
verses.king_james: Then said the Lord, Doest thou well to be angry?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressus est Jonas de civitate, et sedit contra orientem civitatis : et fecit sibimet umbraculum ibi, et sedebat subter illud in umbra, donec videret quid accideret civitati.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֤א יוֹנָה֙ מִן־הָעִ֔יר וַיֵּ֖שֶׁב מִקֶּ֣דֶם לָעִ֑יר וַיַּ֩עַשׂ֩ ל֨וֹ שָׁ֜ם סֻכָּ֗ה וַיֵּ֤שֶׁב תַּחְתֶּ֙יהָ֙ בַּצֵּ֔ל עַ֚ד אֲשֶׁ֣ר יִרְאֶ֔ה מַה־יִּהְיֶ֖ה בָּעִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Jonah went out of the city, and sat toward the east side of the city: and he made himself a booth there, and he sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would befall the city.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jona läksi kaupungista ja istui kaupungista itään päin, ja teki siellä itsellensä majan ja istui sen alla varjossa, siihenasti kuin hän näkis, mitä kaupungille tapahtuis.
verses.king_james: So Jonah went out of the city, and sat on the east side of the city, and there made him a booth, and sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would become of the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præparavit Dominus Deus hederam, et ascendit super caput Jonæ, ut esset umbra super caput ejus, et protegeret eum (laboraverat enim) : et lætatus est Jonas super hedera lætitia magna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְמַ֣ן יְהֹוָֽה־אֱ֠לֹהִ֠ים קִיקָי֞וֹן וַיַּ֣עַל ׀ מֵעַ֣ל לְיוֹנָ֗ה לִֽהְי֥וֹת צֵל֙ עַל־רֹאשׁ֔וֹ לְהַצִּ֥יל ל֖וֹ מֵרָֽעָת֑וֹ וַיִּשְׂמַ֥ח יוֹנָ֛ה עַל־הַקִּֽיקָי֖וֹן שִׂמְחָ֥ה גְדוֹלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord God prepared an ivy, and it came up over the head of Jonah, to be a shadow over his head, and to cover him (for he was fatigued): and Jonah was exceeding glad of the ivy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra Jumala toimitti yhden pensaan, joka kasvoi Jonan ylitse, että se olis hänen päänsä varjonnut, ja virvoittanut häntä hänen vaivassansa; ja Jona riemuitsi siitä pensaasta suurella ilolla.
verses.king_james: And the Lord God prepared a gourd, and made it to come up over Jonah, that it might be a shadow over his head, to deliver him from his grief. So Jonah was exceeding glad of the gourd.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et paravit Deus vermen ascensu diluculi in crastinum : et percussit hederam, et exaruit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְמַ֤ן הָֽאֱלֹהִים֙ תּוֹלַ֔עַת בַּעֲל֥וֹת הַשַּׁ֖חַר לַֽמׇּחֳרָ֑ת וַתַּ֥ךְ אֶת־הַקִּֽיקָי֖וֹן וַיִּיבָֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But God prepared a worm, when the morning arose on the following day: and it struck the ivy and it withered.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra toimitti madon huomeneltain, kuin aamurusko koitti, joka söi sitä pensasta, että se kuivettui.
verses.king_james: But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day, and it smote the gourd that it withered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum ortus fuisset sol, præcepit Dominus vento calido et urenti : et percussit sol super caput Jonæ, et æstuabat : et petivit animæ suæ ut moreretur, et dixit : Melius est mihi mori quam vivere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י ׀ כִּזְרֹ֣חַ הַשֶּׁ֗מֶשׁ וַיְמַ֨ן אֱלֹהִ֜ים ר֤וּחַ קָדִים֙ חֲרִישִׁ֔ית וַתַּ֥ךְ הַשֶּׁ֛מֶשׁ עַל־רֹ֥אשׁ יוֹנָ֖ה וַיִּתְעַלָּ֑ף וַיִּשְׁאַ֤ל אֶת־נַפְשׁוֹ֙ לָמ֔וּת וַיֹּ֕אמֶר ט֥וֹב מוֹתִ֖י מֵחַיָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the sun was risen, the Lord commanded a hot and burning wind: and the sun beat upon the head of Jonah, and he broiled with the heat: and he desired for his soul that he might die, and said: It is better for me to die than to live.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin aurinko noussut oli, toimitti Jumala suuren itätuulen, ja aurinko paisti Jonan päähän, että hän tuli voimattomaksi. Niin hän toivotti sielullensa kuolemaa, ja sanoi: minä kuolisin ennenkuin minä eläisin.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when the sun did arise, that God prepared a vehement east wind; and the sun beat upon the head of Jonah, that he fainted, and wished in himself to die, and said, It is better for me to die than to live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus ad Jonam : Putasne bene irasceris tu super hedera ? Et dixit : Bene irascor ego usque ad mortem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֱלֹהִים֙ אֶל־יוֹנָ֔ה הַהֵיטֵ֥ב חָרָֽה־לְךָ֖ עַל־הַקִּֽיקָי֑וֹן וַיֹּ֕אמֶר הֵיטֵ֥ב חָֽרָה־לִ֖י עַד־מָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to Jonah: Dost thou think thou hast reason to be angry, for the ivy? And he said: I am angry with reason even unto death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jumala sanoi Jonalle: luuletkos oikein vihastunees siitä pensaasta? Ja hän sanoi: minä vihastun hamaan kuolemaan asti.
verses.king_james: And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the gourd? And he said, I do well to be angry, even unto death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus : Tu doles super hederam in qua non laborasti, neque fecisti ut cresceret ; quæ sub una nocte nata est, et sub una nocte periit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהֹוָ֔ה אַתָּ֥ה חַ֙סְתָּ֙ עַל־הַקִּ֣יקָי֔וֹן אֲשֶׁ֛ר לֹא־עָמַ֥לְתָּ בּ֖וֹ וְלֹ֣א גִדַּלְתּ֑וֹ שֶׁבִּן־לַ֥יְלָה הָיָ֖ה וּבִן־לַ֥יְלָה אָבָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said: Thou art grieved for the ivy, for which thou hast not laboured, nor made it to grow, which in one night came up, and in one night perished.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi: sinä armahdat pensasta, josta et mitään vaivaa nähnyt, etkä sitä kasvattanut, joka ynnä kasvoi ja ynnä katosi:
verses.king_james: Then said the Lord, Thou hast had pity on the gourd, for the which thou hast not laboured, neither madest it grow; which came up in a night, and perished in a night:

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego non parcam Ninive, civitati magnæ, in qua sunt plus quam centum viginti millia hominum qui nesciunt quid sit inter dexteram et sinistram suam, et jumenta multa ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽאֲנִי֙ לֹ֣א אָח֔וּס עַל־נִינְוֵ֖ה הָעִ֣יר הַגְּדוֹלָ֑ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר יֶשׁ־בָּ֡הּ הַרְבֵּה֩ מִֽשְׁתֵּים־עֶשְׂרֵ֨ה רִבּ֜וֹ אָדָ֗ם אֲשֶׁ֤ר לֹֽא־יָדַע֙ בֵּין־יְמִינ֣וֹ לִשְׂמֹאל֔וֹ וּבְהֵמָ֖ה רַבָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And shall I not spare Ninive, that great city, in which there are more than a hundred and twenty thousand persons, that know how to distinguish between their right hand and their left, and many beasts?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö minun pitänyt armahtaman suurta kaupunkin Niniveä, jossa on enempi kuin sata tuhatta ja kaksikymmentä tuhatta ihmistä, jotka ei tiedä eroittaa kumpi oikia eli kumpi vasen käsi on, niin myös paljo eläimiä?
verses.king_james: And should not I spare Nineveh, that great city, wherein are more than sixscore thousand persons that cannot discern between their right hand and their left hand; and also much cattle?
